---
title: "Modeling"
author: "Sadie Kasten"
date: "2026-04-05"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# setup
```{r}
library(here)
library(tseries)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(cowplot)
library(forecast)

```

# Load in the data
```{r}
#load in data
#ignore my wd. chunk was overriding what I set in the console, # out if not Sadie
setwd("/Users/sadiekasten/Desktop/Time Series/R files/final proj")
raw_Std <- read.csv(file="data/processed/Std_household_load.csv",
                            header=TRUE) %>%
  mutate(datetime = ymd_hms(datetime_utc, tz = "UTC")) %>% 
  mutate(cluster = as.character(cluster)) %>% 
  select("datetime","cluster","load_kwh") %>% 
  mutate(cluster = paste("Std",cluster))


raw_ToU <- read.csv(file="data/processed/ToU_household_load.csv",
                            header=TRUE) %>%
  mutate(datetime = ymd_hms(datetime_utc, tz = "UTC")) %>% 
  mutate(cluster = as.character(cluster)) %>% 
  select("datetime","cluster","load_kwh") %>% 
  mutate(cluster = paste("ToU",cluster))
```

# Visualize the raw data

```{r}

raw_all <- rbind(raw_Std,raw_ToU)

#raw visualization
ggplot(raw_all)+
  geom_line(aes(x = datetime, y = load_kwh,color = cluster), alpha = 0.6)+
  labs(x = "Date and Time", y = "Load (kWh)")+
  ggtitle("Standard and Time of Use Demand Clustered Households, 2012 and 2013")+
  theme_bw()

#2012 visualization
raw_Std_2012 <- raw_Std %>% 
  filter(year(datetime)==2012)

raw_ToU_2012 <- raw_ToU %>% 
  filter(year(datetime)==2012)

raw_all_2012 <- rbind(raw_Std_2012,raw_ToU_2012)

ggplot(raw_all_2012)+
  geom_line(aes(x = datetime, y = load_kwh,color = cluster), alpha = 0.5)+
  labs(x = "Date and Time", y = "Load (kWh)")+
  ggtitle("Standard and Time of Use Demand Clustered Households, 2012")+
  theme_bw()
```

#Process data

```{r}
processed_Std_2012 <- raw_Std %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cluster,
              values_from = load_kwh) %>% 
  select("datetime","Std 1","Std 2") %>% 
  filter(year(datetime) == 2012)

processed_ToU_2012 <- raw_ToU %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cluster,
              values_from = load_kwh) %>% 
  select("datetime","ToU 1","ToU 2") %>% 
  filter(year(datetime) == 2012)

processed_Std_2013 <- raw_Std %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cluster,
              values_from = load_kwh) %>% 
  select("datetime","Std 1","Std 2") %>% 
  filter(year(datetime) == 2013)

processed_ToU_2013 <- raw_ToU %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cluster,
              values_from = load_kwh) %>% 
  select("datetime","ToU 1","ToU 2") %>% 
  filter(year(datetime) == 2013)

```

#Convert to ts

```{r}

#2012
ts_Std1_2012 <- msts(processed_Std_2012$`Std 1`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(24,168,8766),
                            start=c(2012,1,0)) 

ts_Std2_2012 <- msts(processed_Std_2012$`Std 2`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(24,168,8766),
                            start=c(2012,1,0)) 

ts_ToU1_2012 <- msts(processed_ToU_2012$`ToU 1`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(24,168,8766),
                            start=c(2012,1,0)) 

ts_ToU2_2012 <- msts(processed_ToU_2012$`ToU 2`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(24,168,8766),
                            start=c(2012,1,0)) 

#2013

ts_Std1_2013 <- msts(processed_Std_2013$`Std 1`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(24,168,8766),
                            start=c(2013,1,0)) 

ts_Std2_2013 <- msts(processed_Std_2013$`Std 2`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(24,168,8766),
                            start=c(2013,1,0)) 

ts_ToU1_2013 <- msts(processed_ToU_2013$`ToU 1`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(24,168,8766),
                            start=c(2013,1,0)) 

ts_ToU2_2013 <- msts(processed_ToU_2013$`ToU 2`, 
                            seasonal.periods =c(24,168,8766),
                            start=c(2013,1,0))

```

# Decomposition

```{r}
#std1 decomposition
Std1_2012_decomp <- ts_Std1_2012 %>% mstl()

#std2 decomp
Std2_2012_decomp <- ts_Std2_2012 %>% mstl() 

#tou1 decomposition
ToU1_2012_decomp <- ts_ToU1_2012 %>% mstl() 

#tou2 decomp
ToU2_2012_decomp <- ts_ToU2_2012 %>% mstl() 
```

# ACF and PACF 

```{r}

plot_grid(
  autoplot(Acf(Std1_2012_decomp[,"Remainder"], lag.max = 60,plot = FALSE), main ="Std1 Decomp Residuals, 2012"),
  autoplot(Pacf(Std1_2012_decomp[,"Remainder"], lag.max = 60,plot = FALSE), main = "")
)

plot_grid(
  autoplot(Acf(Std2_2012_decomp[,"Remainder"], lag.max = 60,plot = FALSE), main ="Std2 Decomp Residuals, 2012"),
  autoplot(Pacf(Std2_2012_decomp[,"Remainder"], lag.max = 60,plot = FALSE), main = "")
)

plot_grid(
  autoplot(Acf(ToU1_2012_decomp[,"Remainder"], lag.max = 60,plot = FALSE), main ="ToU1 Decomp Residuals, 2012"),
  autoplot(Pacf(ToU1_2012_decomp[,"Remainder"], lag.max = 60,plot = FALSE), main = "")
)

plot_grid(
  autoplot(Acf(ToU2_2012_decomp[,"Remainder"], lag.max = 60,plot = FALSE), main ="ToU2 Decomp Residuals, 2012"),
  autoplot(Pacf(ToU2_2012_decomp[,"Remainder"], lag.max = 60,plot = FALSE), main = "")
)

```


#Advanced seasonality models

```{r}
#training subset, using December of 2012, hourly (744)
n_dec = 744
ts_Std1_2012_train <- subset(ts_Std1_2012, end = length(ts_Std1_2012)-n_dec)

#create a subset for testing purpose
ts_Std1_2012_test <- subset(ts_Std1_2012, start = length(ts_Std1_2012)-n_dec)

Std1_2012_train_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2012_train)
Std1_2012_test_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2012_test)
Std1_2012_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2012)

Std1_training_plot <- plot_grid(Std1_2012_train_plot,Std1_2012_test_plot,Std1_2012_plot)

ggsave("figures/Std1_training_plot.png", plot = Std1_training_plot,width = 4, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
#ARIMA + Fourier Terms for Std1 2012 training time series
#Annual, weekly, and daily seasonality likely for electricity load data.
#hourly load forecasting
#using less than a year of data to test, not going to include yearly K value >1

ARIMA_Std1_train <- auto.arima(ts_Std1_2012_train, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_Std1_2012_train,  K=c(5,3,1)))
AIC(ARIMA_Std1_train)

#Forecast the ARIMA model
#h = 744 hours of December
ARIMAF_Std1_train_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_Std1_train,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_Std1_2012_train, K=c(5,3,1),h=744), h=744) 

#Plot forecast
Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_plot <- autoplot(ARIMAF_Std1_train_forecast) + ylab("Load (kWh)")

ggsave("figures/Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_plot.png", plot = Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_plot,width = 4, height = 5, dpi = 300)

#Plot forecast on top of actual
Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_actual_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2012) +
  autolayer(ARIMAF_Std1_train_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)")

ggsave("figures/Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_actual_plot.png", plot = Std1_ARIMA_training_forecast_actual_plot,width = 4, height = 5, dpi = 300)

```

Tried K values (2,2,1=6044), (4,2,1=3760), (5,2,2=892), (5,3,1=677), (5,3,2=895), and (5,3,3=883) on Std1. Had to input a K value for yearly seasonality because it was specified in the time series for the function to run. The lowest AIC value was (5,3,1) at 677.7, representing daily, weekly, yearly K values. Accepted a '0' as a K value but im not sure what that means so did not use.

Forecast lower than actual, might not be a great model. However, will use this generically for now to forecast 2013 using 2012 data for all 4 groups and compare to actual for 2013.


```{r}
#ARIMA + Fourier Terms for all 4 groups
# 2013 had 8760 hours 

#Std1
ARIMA_Std1 <- auto.arima(ts_Std1_2012, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_Std1_2012,  K=c(5,3,1)))

ARIMA_Std1_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_Std1,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_Std1_2012, K=c(5,3,1),h=8760), h=8760) 

ARIMA_Std1_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2013) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_Std1_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="Forecast vs Actual Std1")

#Std2
ARIMA_Std2 <- auto.arima(ts_Std2_2012, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_Std2_2012,  K=c(5,3,1)))

ARIMA_Std2_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_Std2,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_Std2_2012, K=c(5,3,1),h=8760), h=8760) 

ARIMA_Std2_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std2_2013) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_Std2_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="Forecast vs Actual Std2")

#ToU1
ARIMA_ToU1 <- auto.arima(ts_ToU1_2012, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_ToU1_2012,  K=c(5,3,1)))

ARIMA_ToU1_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_ToU1,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_ToU1_2012, K=c(5,3,1),h=8760), h=8760) 

ARIMA_ToU1_plot <- autoplot(ts_ToU1_2013) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_ToU1_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="Forecast vs Actual ToU1")

#ToU2
ARIMA_ToU2 <- auto.arima(ts_ToU2_2012, seasonal=FALSE, lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_ToU2_2012,  K=c(5,3,1)))

ARIMA_ToU2_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_ToU2,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_ToU2_2012, K=c(5,3,1),h=8760), h=8760) 

ARIMA_ToU2_plot <- autoplot(ts_ToU2_2013) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_ToU2_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="Forecast vs Actual ToU2")

```

```{r}
#ARIMA Plot visualization

ARIMA_F_2013_plots <- plot_grid(ARIMA_Std1_plot,
          ARIMA_Std2_plot,
          ARIMA_ToU1_plot,
          ARIMA_ToU2_plot,
          nrow =2, ncol = 2)

ggsave("figures/ARIMA_F_2013_plots.png", plot = ARIMA_F_2013_plots,width = 4, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

One thing I am curious about: we were hoping that ToU forecasts would be higher than actual. Not the case based on the ARIMA models, however, there did not appear to be much change in the original plot of all 4 groups between 2012 and 2013 for the ToU groups anyway...


```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#TBATS for Std1
TBATS_Std1 <- tbats(ts_Std1_2012)

TBATS_Std1_forecast <- forecast(TBATS_Std1, h=8760)

Std1_TBATS_2013_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2013) +
  autolayer(TBATS_Std1_forecast, series="TBATS",PI=FALSE)+
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="TBATS Std1")

ggsave("figures/Std1_TBATS_2013_plot.png", plot = Std1_TBATS_2013_plot,width = 4, height = 5, dpi = 300)

```

Produced a straight line - not great. Will use the training data to see if shorter time horizons work better.

```{r}
#TBATS for Std1 2012 training
TBATS_Std1_train <- tbats(ts_Std1_2012_train,
  seasonal.periods = c(24, 168, 8760))

TBATS_Std1_forecast_train <- forecast(TBATS_Std1_train,seasonal.periods = c(24, 168, 8760), h=744)

Std1_TBATS_training_forecast_plot <- autoplot(ts_Std1_2012) +
  autolayer(TBATS_Std1_forecast_train, series="TBATS",PI=FALSE)+
  ylab("Load (kWh)") + labs(title="TBATS Std1 2012 Training")

ggsave("figures/Std1_TBATS_training_forecast_plot.png", plot = Std1_TBATS_training_forecast_plot,width = 4, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

Not better at representing the daily seasonality that goes on, spiking the load.



```{r}
#Prophet
```
